meta.title: L550 Engine Replacement Cost UK — Compare Quotes
meta.description: Every Land Rover Discovery Sport L550 engine – 2.2 Duratorq, 2.0 Ingenium diesel and petrol. Real UK costs and honest reliability data. Get free quotes from 12 vetted Land Rover specialists today.

@graph.name: Land Rover Discovery Sport L550 Engine Codes
Land Rover Discovery Sport L550 Core Variants

mainEntity.name: How much does a Land Rover Discovery Sport L550 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned L550 engine typically costs £2,000–£7,500 supply only [EM-QUOTE], depending on the engine – the volume 204DTD diesel sits at £3,500–£5,800, while the rare P300e hybrid is higher. Fitting adds £650–£910 at independent specialists. Use our calculator for a personalised figure.

mainEntity.name: Which L550 engine is the most reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The 204PT (Ingenium 2.0 petrol) – it lacks the diesel's timing chain tensioner issues, has no DPF or oil-dilution problems, and its main weakness (water pump) is a manageable £400–£800 repair. The early 224DT Ford-sourced diesel is also reliable but harder to find.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Discovery Sport L550?

acceptedAnswer.text: Timing chain stretch on pre-2019 204DTD diesel engines, water pump failure on the 204PT petrol, EGR cooler failure on the early 224DT Ford unit, and DPF blockage / oil dilution on the Ingenium diesel when used primarily in urban stop-start traffic.

mainEntity.name: Is the Discovery Sport L550 reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: It depends heavily on the engine and year. Pre-2019 204DTD diesels carry a significant timing chain risk – avoid without documented chain history. Post-2019 diesels are improved but still need strict servicing. The petrol 204PT is solid, and the early 224DT is the most dependable diesel (but rare). Choose wisely.

mainEntity.name: Which L550 engine should I avoid?

acceptedAnswer.text: A pre-2019 204DTD diesel without a documented timing-chain replacement is the one purchase we consistently advise against – failure can occur as early as 40,000 miles. The P300e hybrid is still unproven in the long term and its replacement cost is high, so buy with caution.

mainEntity.name: How do I know if my L550 engine needs replacing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Cold-start rattle (204DTD/204DTA), persistent coolant loss (204PT), or a 'Red DPF Full' warning combined with rough running (any diesel) are the key warning signs. Use our diagnostic calculator for a personalised assessment.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Discovery Sport L550 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Apply the 60% Land Rover scrap threshold. Against a well-kept £15,000 L550, a £5,800 204DTD recon is ~39% – safe. Against a tired £8,000 example, the same recon is ~73% – walk away. Always value the car first; if the lowest viable replacement exceeds 60% of market value, scrap or part-ex is wiser.

mainEntity.name: How long do L550 engines last?

acceptedAnswer.text: The 224DT Ford unit can exceed 200,000 miles with care. The 204PT petrol is good for 150,000+ if the cooling system is maintained. The 204DTD diesel's lifespan is limited by the timing chain – addressed proactively, it can reach 120,000+; ignored, it rarely sees 80,000.

mainEntity.name: What is the difference between the Discovery Sport L550 and other Land Rover models like the Evoque?

acceptedAnswer.text: Both share the JLR D8/PTA platforms and many engines, but the L550 is the Discovery-branded 5+2-seat SUV, while the Evoque L551 is a sleeker 5-seat coupe-SUV. Labour access and engine power outputs can vary between models – always check the specific chassis page for exact fitment and costs.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my L550 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate (visible through the windscreen) and vehicle documentation; block stamping is visible from below on the Ingenium units. Tell us your variant and registration year (pre-/post-2019 facelift) and we'll identify it – 12 vetted specialists will confirm it for free.

hero.tagPill: Land Rover Discovery Sport L550 • SUV • 2014–present
hero.h1: Land Rover Discovery Sport L550 Engines — The Complete UK Guide
hero.subHeadline: Every engine fitted to the Discovery Sport L550 – from the Ford-sourced 2.2-litre diesel to the Ingenium 2.0-litre four-cylinder family, both diesel and petrol. Real UK replacement costs, honest failure data, and the timing chain tensioner weakness that still haunts early Ingenium units. Backed by 362+ real UK L550 enquiries in 2025 [EM-VERIFIED].

engineDatabase.h2: Land Rover Discovery Sport L550 Engine Codes — The Complete Database
engineDatabase.subHeadline: Every engine fitted to the Discovery Sport L550 – from the early Ford-sourced units to the full Ingenium family, diesel, petrol and PHEV.

overview.h2: Land Rover Discovery Sport L550 — Overview
overview.intro: The Discovery Sport L550, launched in late 2014 as the Freelander's replacement, is Land Rover's compact luxury crossover SUV built on the LR-MS (D8) platform. It introduced the Discovery name to a new segment, offering five or seven seats and a transverse-engine layout that maximised interior space. A significant 2019 facelift moved the L550 to the JLR PTA platform and introduced mild-hybrid technology across the Ingenium range.
overview.keyFacts: Platform: JLR D8 (2014–2019) / JLR PTA (2019–present) • Predecessor: Freelander 2 (L359) • Successor: Still in production (L550 continues) • Sibling body styles in this generation: None (L550 is the sole Discovery Sport body code)
overview.marketIntelligenceLine: The L550 generated 362 real UK engine enquiries in 2025 [EM-VERIFIED], with the 2.0-litre Ingenium diesel accounting for the majority of demand as these vehicles exit manufacturer warranty.

items.engine: 204DTD (post-2019, revised Ingenium D) – "The revised timing chain tensioner and upgraded oil pump design make post-2019 204DTD units significantly more dependable than earlier examples. Still needs strict servicing, but the catastrophic failure rate is materially lower."
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want Ingenium diesel economy without the early tensioner gamble.

items.engine: 204PT (Ingenium P) – "The petrol Ingenium is the more reliable of the two – no timing chain tensioner crisis, no oil dilution from DPF regens. Water pump and cooling system are the only real demands."
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want petrol reliability without the diesel's caveats.

items.engine: 204DTD (pre-2019, early Ingenium D) – "Timing chain stretch at 40,000–60,000 miles is this engine's defining risk – cold-start rattle is the warning, and a snapped chain means a £5,800+ replacement. Avoid unless chain history is documented."
items.whoItsFor: only buyers with full service history and documented chain work.

items.engine: 204DTD Timing Chain Failure – "Chain stretch leads to valve-piston contact – the engine is effectively scrap. A full reconditioned replacement at £5,800 is the only fix once the chain has gone."
items.whoItsFor: 204DTD owners who caught the rattle early.

items.engine: 224DT (Ford 2.2 Duratorq) – "Early L550s with the Ford-sourced 2.2 diesel are cheaper to buy, cheaper to insure, and – critically – free of the Ingenium chain question entirely. EGR and turbo are the only real bills."
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want L550 ownership without Ingenium anxiety.

items.engine: 204PT P250 – "The 250 PS petrol Ingenium delivers genuine performance without the diesel's reliability baggage. Most buyers default to diesel, but the petrol is the smarter long-term play."
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want L550 performance without the diesel's caveats.

ownershipEconomics.h2: Land Rover Discovery Sport L550 — Ownership Economics

rows.ownershipVerdict: Good Value – cheap to fix, no chain crisis.
Solid Choice – rare on L550, but reliable.
High Risk – budget for chain or walk away.
Improved – better than early reputation suggests.
Best Petrol – reliable, no DPF/diesel issues.
Specialist – hybrid complexity, high replacement cost.

economicsRule.title: The L550 rule
economicsRule.text: A £3,500–£5,800 204DTD recon against a £10,000 pre‑2019 Discovery Sport sits at 35–58% of the car's value. Against a sub‑£8,000 example, that brushes our 60% Land Rover threshold – we'll honestly tell you to walk away from an early Ingenium diesel without chain history. Against a £15,000+ post‑2019 example, the same recon drops to a routine 25–40% – a repair decision, not a financial disaster. Value the car first, then the engine.

keyTakeaways.question: Which L550 Costs Least To Own?
keyTakeaways.answer: 224DT – cheap EGR fixes, no Ingenium chain question.

keyTakeaways.question: Which L550 Costs Most To Own?
keyTakeaways.answer: 204DTD (pre-2019) – chain failure at 40k–60k miles is a £5,800+ bill.

keyTakeaways.question: Which L550 Is Best Value?
keyTakeaways.answer: 204PT petrol – reliable, no DPF, no chain crisis, and cheaper than you think.

problems.title: 204DTD Ingenium Diesel Timing Chain Stretch
problems.rootCause: The timing chain is mounted on the flywheel side (rear of engine), making it labour‑intensive to access. The hydraulic tensioner fails to maintain proper chain tension, plastic chain guides wear and break apart, and oil starvation from the Ingenium's oil pump design accelerates wear. Cold‑start rattle for 1–2 seconds is the classic early warning.
problems.recommendation: At the first sign of cold‑start rattle, get the chain inspected immediately – a £1,200–£2,500 preventative repair is dramatically cheaper than the £3,500–£5,800 replacement that follows if ignored. On a pre‑2019 L550 worth under £10,000, this decision alone determines whether the car stays economically viable. Post‑2019 units have revised tensioners – still watch, but less prone.

cta.label: Compare 204DTD engine prices for your Discovery Sport L550 →

problems.title: 204DTD DPF Blockage & Oil Dilution
problems.affectedModels: L550 2.0 D150, D165, D180 (all years, urban‑use vehicles).
problems.rootCause: The Ingenium diesel's DPF regeneration cycle is sensitive to urban driving patterns – repeated short trips prevent full regen, leading to soot buildup and the 'Red DPF Full' warning. Unburned fuel from failed regens dilutes the engine oil, reducing lubrication and accelerating chain wear.
problems.recommendation: If your L550 is used primarily in town, force a DPF regen every 500 miles and change oil at 6,000‑mile intervals – not 12,000. A £400–£1,000 DPF service is cheap insurance against the £3,500+ replacement that follows oil‑starvation damage.

cta.label: Compare DPF repair costs for your Discovery Sport L550 →

problems.title: 204PT Ingenium Petrol Water Pump Failure
problems.rootCause: The water pump and plastic cooling system fittings degrade with heat cycling – coolant leaks are the first sign, followed by overheating if ignored.
problems.recommendation: Replace the water pump proactively at 60,000 miles – £400–£800 now is far cheaper than the £3,000+ bill that follows an overheat. This is the single most important maintenance item on the petrol Ingenium.

cta.label: Compare 204PT engine prices for your Discovery Sport L550 →

problems.title: 224DT Ford Duratorq EGR Cooler & Turbo Actuator
problems.rootCause: The Ford‑sourced 2.2 diesel's EGR cooler leaks coolant into the exhaust, and the turbo actuator fails due to carbon buildup – both well‑understood Ford patterns, not Ingenium-specific.
problems.recommendation: This is cheap insurance on the L550's most dependable engine – a £500–£1,200 EGR and turbo check every service is trivial against the 224DT's otherwise solid reputation. If you can find an early L550 with the 2.2 Duratorq, buy it – it's the one without the Ingenium chain question.

cta.label: Compare 224DT engine prices for your Discovery Sport L550 →
